title: jasione humilis var. maritima
intro: Jasione humilis var. maritima, also known as Sea Bluebells, is a subspecies of the Jasione humilis plant, which is a low-growing, flowering perennial native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Jasione humilis var. maritima include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into ointments and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
